reasoning: Despite his notable history of concussions—including one in September 2024 (Week 2 of the 2024 season)—and a late-season hip injury that caused him to miss the final two games of the 2024 season, Tua Tagovailoa has firmly and consistently stated that he has no plans to retire. In January 2025, his father recounted that Tua told him he would rather 'die on the field' than retire. Furthermore, Tua has a massive incentive to keep playing: he stands to make more than $50 million in guaranteed money in 2025 that he would forfeit by retiring. Recent media coverage from April and May 2025 indicates that he continues to participate in the Dolphins' offseason workout programs and is preparing to be the team's starter when the 2025 regular season begins. With no recent setbacks reported during the 2025 spring programs and overwhelming financial and personal motivations pushing him to stay on the field, the likelihood of him retiring before September 2025 is very low. Setting at 0.05 to comply with probability limits.
